questions.question: A clinic tests patients for two independent conditions. The probability of testing positive for condition A is 0.3, and for condition B is 0.4. What is the probability of testing positive for BOTH?
questions.correct_detail: For two independent events, P(A AND B) = P(A) × P(B). P(A) = 0.3, P(B) = 0.4. P(both) = 0.3 × 0.4 = 0.12. Answer: 0.12 (12%). This is the multiplication rule for independent events — the outcome of one test does not affect the other. Common error: adding the probabilities (0.3 + 0.4 = 0.7) gives P(A OR B), not P(A AND B). The union formula for OR is: P(A OR B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A AND B) = 0.3 + 0.4 - 0.12 = 0.58.

questions.question: A bag contains 4 red, 6 blue, and 10 green balls. What is the probability of NOT picking a green ball?

options.C: 0.60 — probability of picking a blue or red ball combined
options.D: 0.30 — probability of picking a red ball only

questions.correct_detail: Step 1: Total balls = 4 + 6 + 10 = 20. Step 2: P(green) = 10/20 = 0.5. Step 3: Use the complement rule: P(NOT green) = 1 - P(green) = 1 - 0.5 = 0.5. Answer: 0.5. Non-green balls = 4 red + 6 blue = 10 out of 20, confirming P(not green) = 10/20 = 0.5. The complement rule (P(not A) = 1 - P(A)) is often the fastest approach when there are many outcomes to count.

questions.question: A health centre's monthly budget is $B. After spending $1,200 on salaries and a third of the remainder on supplies, $800 is left. What was the original budget?

options.C: $2,800 (forgot to account for the third share)
options.D: $3,200 (divided remainder by half instead of third)

questions.correct_detail: Work backwards from what's left. After salaries: remaining = B - 1,200. One third of remainder spent on supplies, so two thirds remain: (2/3)(B - 1,200) = 800. Multiply both sides by 3/2: B - 1,200 = 800 × 3/2 = 1,200. Therefore B = 1,200 + 1,200 = $3,000. Answer: $3,000. Check: Start with $3,000. After salaries: $1,800 left. One third on supplies = $600. Remaining = $1,800 - $600 = $1,200... Let us re-verify: (2/3)(3000-1200) = (2/3)(1800) = 1200 ≠ 800. Correcting: (2/3)(B-1200)=800 → B-1200=1200 → B=2400. Check: (2/3)(2400-1200)=(2/3)(1200)=800 ✓. Correct answer is $2,400 — option A. Note: always verify by substituting back.

questions.question: A vaccination team needs to cover 3,600 households. Team A can visit 45 households per day and Team B can visit 30 households per day. Working together, how many days will it take?

options.C: 40 days (used only Team A's rate alone)
options.D: 80 days (used only Team B's rate alone)

questions.correct_detail: Combined rate = 45 + 30 = 75 households per day. Days needed = 3,600 / 75 = 48 days. Answer: 48 days. This is a combined work rate problem. The key step is adding the rates (not the times). Check: Team A in 48 days: 45 × 48 = 2,160. Team B in 48 days: 30 × 48 = 1,440. Total: 2,160 + 1,440 = 3,600 ✓. This type of calculation is used in planning immunisation campaigns, outreach schedules, and field survey timelines.

questions.question: A country has 50,000 births per year. The under-5 mortality rate is 60 per 1,000 live births. How many children under 5 die each year?
questions.correct_detail: Deaths = (rate per 1,000 / 1,000) × number of live births. Step 1: Rate = 60 per 1,000 = 0.06 (6%). Step 2: Deaths = 0.06 × 50,000 = 3,000 children per year. Answer: 3,000. Alternatively: 60/1,000 × 50,000 = 60 × 50 = 3,000. Under-5 mortality rate (U5MR) is one of the most important indicators of child health and overall development — a U5MR of 60/1,000 is typical of a lower-middle-income country. The global average is approximately 37/1,000 (2023).

questions.question: In a health survey, the mean systolic blood pressure of a group is 125 mmHg with a standard deviation of 10 mmHg. What does the standard deviation tell us?

options.A: The average blood pressure across the group is 10 mmHg
options.B: Most readings fall between 115 and 135 mmHg — showing how spread out the values are around the mean
options.C: The maximum blood pressure recorded was 135 mmHg
options.D: 10% of people have abnormal blood pressure

questions.correct_detail: Standard deviation (SD) measures the spread of data around the mean. An SD of 10 mmHg means most values (approximately 68% in a normal distribution) fall within one SD of the mean — i.e. between 125 - 10 = 115 mmHg and 125 + 10 = 135 mmHg. A small SD means values are clustered tightly around the mean; a large SD means they are widely spread. SD does NOT mean the average is 10 or that the max is 135 — it describes variability, not a boundary.

questions.question: Two clinics measure patient waiting times. Clinic A: mean = 20 min, SD = 2 min. Clinic B: mean = 20 min, SD = 15 min. What does this tell us?

options.A: Clinic B is faster on average than Clinic A
options.B: Clinic A has more consistent waiting times; Clinic B is far more variable
options.C: Both clinics perform identically since their means are the same
options.D: Clinic A has more patients than Clinic B

questions.correct_detail: Both clinics have the same mean waiting time (20 minutes), but very different standard deviations. Clinic A (SD = 2) has very consistent times — most patients wait close to 20 minutes. Clinic B (SD = 15) is highly variable — some patients may wait only 5 minutes while others wait 35 minutes or more. The mean alone does not tell the full story; SD reveals the reliability and consistency of a service. In public health, high variability in care delivery often indicates inequity or system inefficiency.

questions.question: A dataset of children's heights (cm) has a mean of 110 cm and SD of 5 cm. Approximately what percentage of children fall between 100 cm and 120 cm?
questions.correct_detail: Using the 68-95-99.7 rule (Empirical Rule) for normal distributions: 68% of values fall within 1 SD of the mean (105–115 cm here). 95% fall within 2 SDs of the mean. 99.7% fall within 3 SDs. Here: 100 cm = 110 - 2×5 = mean - 2 SD. 120 cm = 110 + 2×5 = mean + 2 SD. So the range 100–120 cm represents mean ± 2 SD → approximately 95% of children. This rule is essential for interpreting population health data and reference ranges.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about standard deviation is TRUE?

options.A: A higher SD always means the data is more accurate
options.C: SD measures how spread out values are around the mean
options.D: SD and mean always have the same numerical value

questions.correct_detail: Standard deviation (SD) measures the dispersion or spread of data values around the mean. Option A is FALSE — a higher SD means more variability, not more accuracy; in fact high variability can indicate inconsistency. Option B is FALSE — SD = 0 when all values are identical (e.g. everyone scored exactly 50). Option D is FALSE — mean and SD are independent measures; they can be any values. SD is one of the most important descriptive statistics in public health and clinical research.
